data.title: CrossFit MINS Workout
data.text: Workout of the Day 26.10.2025 WODDAILYSWEAT For Quality 6 Rounds 3:00 Row/Ski Erg 20 Box Stepups 15 Kettlebell Sumo Deadlift High Pulls (24/16) 10 Ring Rows Sweat: 16/8 Train: 20/12 Compete: 24/16"
data.full_text:
Workout of the Day

26.10.2025

WOD
DAILYSWEAT


For Quality
6 Rounds
3:00 Row/Ski Erg
20 Box Stepups
15 Kettlebell Sumo Deadlift High Pulls (24/16)
10 Ring Rows


Sweat: 16/8
Train: 20/12
Compete: 24/16"

ai_explanation.explanation:
Workout Overview


Hey everyone! Today’s WOD is all about quality movement and building endurance. We’ll be doing 6 rounds of a mix of cardio, strength, and bodyweight movements. The goal here is to maintain good form throughout each round while pushing your limits. Remember, it's not just about finishing fast; it's about doing each movement correctly and efficiently. Let's break it down!


Workout Components


1. Row/Ski Erg (3:00)

Technique Steps:
  • **Rowing**: Sit on the rower, strap your feet in, and grab the handle. Start with your knees bent, back straight, and arms extended.
  • **Drive**: Push through your legs, lean back slightly, and pull the handle towards your chest.
  • **Recovery**: Extend your arms, lean forward, and bend your knees to return to the starting position.

  • **Ski Erg**: Stand tall, grab the handles, and start with your arms extended overhead.
  • **Pull**: Engage your core and pull the handles down towards your hips, using your legs to assist.
  • **Return**: Let the handles return to the starting position while keeping your core engaged.

    • Form Tips:
  • Keep your back straight and avoid hunching over.
  • Use your legs to generate power, not just your arms.
  • Maintain a steady rhythm; don’t rush!

    • Scaling/Modifications:
  • If you're new to rowing or skiing, consider reducing the time to 2:00.
  • Focus on technique rather than speed; it’s okay to take breaks if needed.

    • 2. Box Step-Ups (20 reps)

    Technique Steps:
  • Stand in front of the box with feet hip-width apart.
  • Step up with one foot, pressing through your heel, and bring the other foot up to meet it on the box.
  • Step back down one foot at a time, alternating legs.

    • Form Tips:
  • Keep your chest up and core engaged.
  • Use your legs to lift, not your back.
  • Ensure the entire foot is on the box to avoid slipping.

    • Scaling/Modifications:
  • Use a lower box if you’re not comfortable with the height.
  • If balance is an issue, hold onto a wall or a sturdy object for support.

    • 3. Kettlebell Sumo Deadlift High Pulls (15 reps)

    Technique Steps:
  • Stand with feet wider than shoulder-width, kettlebell between your feet.
  • Hinge at the hips to grab the kettlebell with both hands.
  • Drive through your heels, extending your hips and knees, and pull the kettlebell up to your chin, keeping elbows high.

    • Form Tips:
  • Keep your back flat and chest up during the hinge.
  • Use your legs to initiate the movement, not just your arms.
  • Control the kettlebell on the way down to avoid injury.

    • Scaling/Modifications:
  • Use a lighter kettlebell if you’re new to this movement.
  • If kettlebells are unavailable, you can use dumbbells or perform a bodyweight squat instead.

    • 4. Ring Rows (10 reps)

    Technique Steps:
  • Set the rings at a height where you can comfortably reach them while standing.
  • Grab the rings with an overhand grip, lean back, and keep your body straight.
  • Pull your chest towards the rings while keeping your elbows close to your body.

    • Form Tips:
  • Engage your core to maintain a straight line from head to heels.
  • Avoid using momentum; focus on controlled movements.
  • Keep your shoulders down and away from your ears.

    • Scaling/Modifications:
  • If rings are too challenging, perform inverted rows on a bar or use a TRX.
  • You can also do bent-over rows with a dumbbell if rings are unavailable.

    • Loading and Rep-Modification Options


  • **Sweat**:
  • Row/Ski: 2:00
  • Box Step-Ups: 16/8 box
  • Kettlebell: 16/8 kg
  • Ring Rows: 8 reps

  • **Train**:
  • Row/Ski: 3:00
  • Box Step-Ups: 20 reps
  • Kettlebell: 20/12 kg
  • Ring Rows: 10 reps

  • **Compete**:
  • Row/Ski: 3:00
  • Box Step-Ups: 20 reps
  • Kettlebell: 24/16 kg
  • Ring Rows: 10 reps

    • Strategy Advice:
  • Focus on maintaining a steady pace throughout the rounds.
  • Break up the reps if needed, especially on the kettlebell pulls and ring rows.
  • Stay hydrated and listen to your body; quality over quantity is key!

    • Alright team, let’s get after it! Remember to encourage each other and have fun while you work hard. You've got this!

combined_content.title: CrossFit MINS Workout
combined_content.workout_text:
WOD:

For Quality

6 Rounds:

  • 3:00 Row/Ski Erg
  • 20 Box Step-ups
  • 15 Kettlebell Sumo Deadlift High Pulls (24/16)
  • 10 Ring Rows

    • Sweat: 16/8

    Train: 20/12

    Compete: 24/16

--- AI EXPLANATION --- ## Workout Overview Hey everyone! Today’s WOD is all about quality movement and building endurance. We’ll be doing 6 rounds of a mix of cardio, strength, and bodyweight movements. The goal here is to maintain good form throughout each round while pushing your limits. Remember, it's not just about finishing fast; it's about doing each movement correctly and efficiently. Let's break it down! ## Workout Components ### 1. Row/Ski Erg (3:00) #### Technique Steps: - **Rowing**: Sit on the rower, strap your feet in, and grab the handle. Start with your knees bent, back straight, and arms extended. - **Drive**: Push through your legs, lean back slightly, and pull the handle towards your chest. - **Recovery**: Extend your arms, lean forward, and bend your knees to return to the starting position. - **Ski Erg**: Stand tall, grab the handles, and start with your arms extended overhead. - **Pull**: Engage your core and pull the handles down towards your hips, using your legs to assist. - **Return**: Let the handles return to the starting position while keeping your core engaged. #### Form Tips: - Keep your back straight and avoid hunching over. - Use your legs to generate power, not just your arms. - Maintain a steady rhythm; don’t rush! #### Scaling/Modifications: - If you're new to rowing or skiing, consider reducing the time to 2:00. - Focus on technique rather than speed; it’s okay to take breaks if needed. ### 2. Box Step-Ups (20 reps) #### Technique Steps: - Stand in front of the box with feet hip-width apart. - Step up with one foot, pressing through your heel, and bring the other foot up to meet it on the box. - Step back down one foot at a time, alternating legs. #### Form Tips: - Keep your chest up and core engaged. - Use your legs to lift, not your back. - Ensure the entire foot is on the box to avoid slipping. #### Scaling/Modifications: - Use a lower box if you’re not comfortable with the height. - If balance is an issue, hold onto a wall or a sturdy object for support. ### 3. Kettlebell Sumo Deadlift High Pulls (15 reps) #### Technique Steps: - Stand with feet wider than shoulder-width, kettlebell between your feet. - Hinge at the hips to grab the kettlebell with both hands. - Drive through your heels, extending your hips and knees, and pull the kettlebell up to your chin, keeping elbows high. #### Form Tips: - Keep your back flat and chest up during the hinge. - Use your legs to initiate the movement, not just your arms. - Control the kettlebell on the way down to avoid injury. #### Scaling/Modifications: - Use a lighter kettlebell if you’re new to this movement. - If kettlebells are unavailable, you can use dumbbells or perform a bodyweight squat instead. ### 4. Ring Rows (10 reps) #### Technique Steps: - Set the rings at a height where you can comfortably reach them while standing. - Grab the rings with an overhand grip, lean back, and keep your body straight. - Pull your chest towards the rings while keeping your elbows close to your body. #### Form Tips: - Engage your core to maintain a straight line from head to heels. - Avoid using momentum; focus on controlled movements. - Keep your shoulders down and away from your ears. #### Scaling/Modifications: - If rings are too challenging, perform inverted rows on a bar or use a TRX. - You can also do bent-over rows with a dumbbell if rings are unavailable. ## Loading and Rep-Modification Options - **Sweat**: - Row/Ski: 2:00 - Box Step-Ups: 16/8 box - Kettlebell: 16/8 kg - Ring Rows: 8 reps - **Train**: - Row/Ski: 3:00 - Box Step-Ups: 20 reps - Kettlebell: 20/12 kg - Ring Rows: 10 reps - **Compete**: - Row/Ski: 3:00 - Box Step-Ups: 20 reps - Kettlebell: 24/16 kg - Ring Rows: 10 reps ### Strategy Advice: - Focus on maintaining a steady pace throughout the rounds. - Break up the reps if needed, especially on the kettlebell pulls and ring rows. - Stay hydrated and listen to your body; quality over quantity is key! Alright team, let’s get after it! Remember to encourage each other and have fun while you work hard. You've got this!
